mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase discount rates for equity valuations, mechanically compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears that systematically depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL and RSAFS signal demand-pull inflation that forces policy tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.01 over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during industrial production declines, or the signal reverses direction in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust labor demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show negative correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 295.0, persistent inflation expectations anchor higher discount rates, systematically suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 295.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between HOUST and GDPC1 growth rates indicates sectoral reallocation that creates near-term earnings uncertainty, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing below the 2017 baseline of 100.0 signals a supply bottleneck that constrains corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the signal fails to maintain statistical significance in walk-forward tests.
